title: GLM-5.2 High/Max Effort-Mapping Hotfix
estimated_hours: 3-5 hours immediate work plus a dated one-week reevaluation
pipeline_rationale: The change affects active global model routing and incurs real-provider validation calls, but does not modify OpenCode application code. Independent validation and a delayed reevaluation are warranted.
skipped_stages: Stage 2 plan review is optional because the user required an execution-ready plan with deterministic checks.
Stage 4 RED test writing is replaced by preserved live baseline evidence and a local transport-shape regression mode.
Stage 6 separate test runner is replaced by the diagnostic script's deterministic local mode and live response-token parser.
test_framework: Python 3 standard library plus OpenCode CLI
summary: Swap GLM-5.2 high/max outbound effort mappings while preserving logical labels, document the token anomaly, validate immediately, and reevaluate after one week.
key_outcomes: Logical high remains the default but maps to the empirically lower-token outbound max value.
Logical max remains opt-in and maps to the empirically higher-token outbound high value.
None remains live-validated at zero reasoning tokens.
A reusable nine-call benchmark supports immediate and one-week comparisons.
risks: Small-sample stochastic variance can invert high/max token ordering.
Logical labels and transport values can be confused during future maintenance.
Config-time changes do not affect already-running OpenCode processes until restart.
dependencies: Active Z.AI Coding Plan credentials for live mode
deliverables: Swapped GLM-5.2 effort mapping in canonical global JSONC
validation_criteria: Local transport capture matches the four-case expected mapping
Immediate and one-week live runs each contain nine successful calls
All none calls report zero reasoning tokens
One-week high-to-max generated-token ratio is calculated and dispositioned
